evidence.text: Increased sales were more heavily weighed towards original equipment sales. evidence.text: Backlog of $2,065.0 million at March 31, 2019 increased by $173.4 million, or 9.2%, as compared with December 31, 2018. Approximately 36% of the backlog at March 31, 2019 was related to aftermarket orders.

factors.summary: The company demonstrates strong pricing power, as evidenced by consistent improvements in gross profit margins across different segments. This is driven by favorable sales mix shifts towards higher margin products, operational efficiency gains, and cost savings initiatives.

evidence.text: The increase in gross profit margin was primarily attributed to the favorable impact of revenue recognized on higher margin projects, sales mix shift to higher margin aftermarket sales and improvements in operational efficiency.
evidence.subsection: Gross Profit and Gross Profit Margin

evidence.text: Gross profit margin for the three months ended March 31, 2019 of 34.6% increased from 31.8% for the same period in 2018. The increase in gross profit margin was primarily attributable to the favorable impact of increased sales on our absorption of fixed manufacturing costs, favorable mix shift within original equipment and decreased charges and increased savings related to our Realignment Programs as compared to the same period in 2018.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Strong pricing power demonstrated by consistent improvements in gross profit margins.
